original_prompt: A realistic stone statue stands motionless in the center of a normal outdoor courtyard. The camera circles clockwise around the statue exactly two complete times, completing a total rotation of 720 degrees. The camera maintains a constant distance and height and returns to its original viewpoint. The statue remains stationary, centered, fully visible, and visually consistent throughout.

reference_attempts.stdout:
All three required outputs already exist from a prior run and check out: h264/yuv420p MP4 at 720x480/8fps (49 frames), and the contact sheet clearly shows the camera orbiting clockwise around the stationary statue through two full 720° revolutions (arm marker sweeping through front/side/back positions twice, identical start/end pose) while the statue itself never rotates. I re-verified the MP4 is valid and readable via imageio_ffmpeg, so no changes were needed.

Validation succeeded. Files present in current directory:
- `statue_orbit_generated_renderer.py`
- `statue_orbit_original.mp4`
- `statue_orbit_check.jpg`

v2v_prompt: A realistic stone statue stands motionless in the center of an outdoor courtyard. The camera circles clockwise around the statue, completing two full 720-degree rotations at constant distance and height, returning to its original viewpoint. The statue remains stationary, centered, fully visible, and visually consistent throughout the entire sequence. Preserve the reference video's exact camera path, subject motion, action order, direction, timing, duration, rotation count, and number of subjects. Keep every subject visually consistent throughout.
negative_prompt: cuts, zoom, fisheye distortion, camera shake, moving statue, rotating statue, changing statue, morphing, duplicated statue, extra people, extra objects, inconsistent courtyard, deformed geometry
